topic: READ-ONLY meta-audit of n6/atlas.append.<slug>.n6 naming convention. 166 shards span manual archive shards (pre-2026-04-26), historical-from-nexus absorption shards (2026-04-26), tool-emitted ω-cycle witness shards (2026-04-26 fanout + 2026-04-27 fanout). Convention drift: ~22% include date suffix (-YYYY-MM-DD), ~78% don't. Cross-session shard re-fire on same topic-slug currently overwrites witness JSON but produces a new shard if slug differs. Document inconsistency, propose convention, defer all renames to user authorization.
rule: raw 23: atlas DSL grammar mini-lint (n/a — no shard edits)

core_5_op_kernel.1_intake_topic: Today's session emitted ~14 shards via tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa; yesterday's emitted ~16 in fanout. Plus 36 manual archive/absorption shards (some dated, some not). Naming inconsistency: e.g. atlas.append.kick-bench-witness-counter-fix.n6 (no date) vs atlas.append.tecsl-archive-2026-04-27.n6 (dated). This affects (a) cross-session re-fire idempotence, (b) ls-sortability, (c) git-blame readability.

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: all-shards-enumerated
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: ls n6/atlas.append.*.n6 count matches inventory length
2_pre_register_falsifiers.method: filesystem glob count vs python inventory

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: each-classified-correctly
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: every shard slug matches exactly one of {dated /^.*-20\d{2}-\d{2}-\d{2}(-cont)?$/, non_dated otherwise}
2_pre_register_falsifiers.method: regex partition; sum should equal total

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: collision-count-honest
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: duplicate slug count reported as exact integer; same-base-with-different-date counted separately as base_slug_collisions

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: orphan-count-honest
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: orphan witnesses (witness JSON in design/kick/ with no matching shard) counted with both raw and slug-normalized (- vs _) checks

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: convention-proposed-with-pros-cons-not-fabricated
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: at least 2 candidate conventions proposed with both pro and con per candidate; no fabricated unanimity; ranked by tradeoff
2_pre_register_falsifiers.method: explicit pros/cons enumeration in proposals field

3_fan_out_axes.rationale: F1+F2+F3+F4 form a closed enumeration. 166 shards == 36 dated + 130 non_dated. 0 slug collisions. 3 base-slug pairs (anima-historical-from-nexus, hexa-lang-historical-from-nexus, n6-architecture-historical-from-nexus) where -cont sibling extends the parent shard within same date. 0 orphan witnesses after slug-normalization (- vs _). 115 shards lack a design/kick/ witness JSON — these include all 36 dated archive/absorption shards (manual emission) and 79 of the older non-dated shards (pre-omega_cycle-witness era OR tool-emitted with witness in subdirs other than design/kick/ such as design/hexa_sim/).

Three candidate conventions evaluated. Each has a real pre-existing pattern in the corpus, so adoption is not greenfield. Tradeoffs explicitly enumerated; no fabricated unanimity. Defer rename burden estimate per candidate.

4_tier_1_promotion_gate_via_falsifier_pa.promotion_criterion: F1 PASS (count matches) + F2 PASS (regex partition closed) + F3 PASS (collision integer honest) + F4 PASS (orphan count honest) for inventory tier; F5 PASS (proposal pros/cons honest) for proposal tier
4_tier_1_promotion_gate_via_falsifier_pa.result: F1 PASS (166==166); F2 PASS (36+130==166); F3 PASS (0 slug collisions, 3 base-slug -cont pairs reported separately); F4 PASS (0 orphan witnesses after norm); F5 PASS (3 candidates with pros+cons documented below)

convention_proposals.name: always-include-date-suffix
pros: Cross-session disambiguation explicit at filename layer (no need to consult git log)
ls n6/atlas.append.*-2026-04-27.n6 instantly enumerates today's shards
Same topic-slug can re-fire on different days without overwriting (eg kick-bench-witness-counter-fix can fire 2026-04-27 and 2026-04-28 producing 2 shards)
Aligns with current 36-shard practice (all archive/absorption shards)
cons: Breaks idempotence on same-day re-fire: emitting twice on 2026-04-27 would still hit same shard path → byte-eq overwrite required (raw 68 honored) but counter-intuitive
Requires retroactive renames for 130 existing non-dated shards if uniformity desired
Date-in-slug duplicates witness JSON's date prefix (eg shard 2026-04-27 + witness 2026-04-27_*) — minor but redundant
If the underlying topic spans multiple days, date-in-name suggests false freshness boundary

convention_proposals.name: exclude-date-from-slug
convention_proposals.pattern: atlas.append.<base-slug>.n6 — date inferred from witness JSON date_prefix or git mtime
pros: Single canonical shard per topic-slug across sessions — explicit byte-eq idempotence (raw 68) on same topic re-fire
Cleaner ls output; topic-grouped not date-grouped
Cross-session evolution of same topic shows in single git log (ie atlas.append.kick-architecture.n6 history shows 2026-04-26 origin + future amends)
cons: Requires git mtime or in-shard timestamp to recover authorship date (already present as 작성: header)

Cross-session rapid topic recurrence loses diff isolation (multiple sessions append into same shard) — for tool-emitted ω-cycle shards this is desirable; for manual archive shards (eg tecsl-archive) re-running on a new corpus means content fully replaced, not merged
Requires retroactive renames for 36 dated shards if uniformity desired
Doesn't address whether the new content should append-to or overwrite existing shard on re-emit (orthogonal policy question)

convention_proposals.name: two-tier-distinction
convention_proposals.pattern: atlas.append.<base-slug>.n6 for tool-emitted ω-cycle witness shards (idempotent, byte-eq); atlas.append.<base-slug>-YYYY-MM-DD.n6 for manual archive/absorption shards (date-stamped, snapshot semantics)
pros: Honors the actual semantic difference: ω-cycle shards are deterministic re-emits of a witness JSON (raw 68 idempotence applies), whereas archive shards are point-in-time corpus snapshots that genuinely differ session-to-session
Approximates current behavior: all 36 dated shards are manual/archive type; most non-dated shards are tool-emitted
Zero rename burden if convention codified as 'going forward' policy with grandfathering
Allows clear policy doc: emit_type=witness => no date; emit_type=archive => date required
cons: Requires shard-emit toolchain to know its emit-type (currently omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa already does — it never adds date; manual scripts add date ad-hoc)
Reviewer must know the dichotomy to read intent from filename

Edge cases: a tool-emitted shard for an archive corpus (e.g. atlas-absorb-uncited-batch-N) — which tier? Currently non-dated, fits witness tier only because the topic embeds 'batch-2-top-100' as discriminator
Two patterns to maintain in any future audit/sweep tooling

convention_recommendation.rationale: Already de facto in place — formalize rather than retrofit. Zero rename burden if grandfathered. Honors raw 68 idempotence for ω-cycle shards while preserving snapshot semantics for archive shards. C1 fails idempotence on same-day re-fire; C2 forces archive shards to lose temporal discriminator (multiple tecsl-archive runs on different corpora would collide).

if_user_authorizes_unification_to_C2.risk: git mv preserves history but breaks any external references (atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, design/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, README links). Audit needed before mass mv.

if_user_authorizes_unification_to_C1.risk: Higher mass-mv risk; 130 git mv operations; many shards have witnesses in design/kick/ that already encode the date in JSON filename — would create date-redundancy at filename and witness-prefix layers.

deferred_to_user_authorization: Whether to formalize C3 as a written convention in atlas.n6 L0 header or a separate doc
Whether to add a lint/audit hexa tool to enforce C3 (eg detect future tool-emitted shards with date suffix or archive shards without)
Whether to retroactively rename any anomalous shards (currently none identified as anomalous under C3)
carry_forward.reason: Read-only meta-audit; convention drift is documented but not pathological — 0 collisions, 0 orphan witnesses (after norm), 3 -cont sibling pairs all intentional

honesty_notes: 115 shards lack a design/kick/ witness JSON. This number includes 36 manual archive shards (no witness expected) plus ~79 older shards whose witness JSONs may live in design/hexa_sim/, design/m3/, etc. — see header_witness_path field for evidence (128/166 shards have an in-header witness path; only 53 of those resolve under design/kick/ specifically).
Original Background note said '~32 expected' shards; actual count is 166. The discrepancy reflects shards added across many prior sessions (not only today + yesterday). All 166 enumerated.

Slug normalization (- vs _) was needed because witness JSON filenames sometimes use _ (eg kick_architecture_omega_cycle.json) while shard slugs use - (eg kick-architecture). Without normalization, false orphan count would inflate.
